title: Space Force completes procurement reorganization, creating nine acquisition portfolios
summary: The U.S. Space Force has completed a procurement reorganization by creating nine acquisition portfolios, each led by portfolio acquisition executives responsible for requirements and modernization decisions across major mission areas.

title: Space Force awards Pulse Space $40 million to advance laser power technology
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded Pulse Space a $40 million contract to develop laser power technology for transmitting power and data between spacecraft. The startup is focused on advancing laser systems for space applications.

title: Golden Dome and the search for new space markets
summary: Golden Dome, a missile-defense program, is attracting space and defense startups seeking new markets despite unresolved details. The initiative is emerging as a key focus for industry growth.

title: Blue Origin seeks to raise $10 billion in outside capital
summary: Blue Origin is seeking to raise $10 billion in external funding to support its expansion in launch services and satellite constellations. This marks the company's first attempt to attract outside investment.

title: MDA Space buys French Earth data analytics company CLS
summary: MDA Space, a Canadian company, has acquired the French Earth data analytics firm CLS, marking its second major acquisition in recent weeks. This deal is part of a broader trend of mergers and acquisitions within the space industry.

title: New Singapore space agency seeks to build up the country’s space industry
summary: Singapore’s new space agency signed a cooperation agreement with Japan to strengthen its space industry. The collaboration aims to accelerate the country's growth in the space sector.

title: ispace to send larger payloads to the moon on SpaceX’s Starship
summary: ispace will utilize SpaceX's Starship for future lunar missions to transport larger payloads to the moon. The Japanese lunar exploration company has secured space on a Starship lander mission.

title: Is this the year for a NASA authorization bill?
summary: The article discusses the possibility of a NASA authorization bill being passed this year, with appropriators expressing optimism about returning to a regular appropriations process.

title: Fi expands Starlink direct-to-device capability into dog-tracking
summary: Fi launched a dog tracker on July 8 that uses T-Mobile’s Starlink-enabled T-Satellite service for nationwide connectivity. This expands Starlink’s direct-to-device capabilities into pet tracking.

title: The satellite industry’s most expensive problem isn’t in orbit
summary: AST SpaceMobile deployed three additional BlueBird satellites into low Earth orbit, aiming to build a space-based cellular broadband network of 45 to 60 satellites. The company is advancing its plans despite industry challenges.

title: The US wants to build offshore rocket launch sites. Critics say 'our coasts deserve better'
summary: The U.S. government is exploring the idea of offshore rocket launch sites for commercial missions, but advocacy groups are concerned about environmental impacts on coastal areas.

title: China announces plan to build early-warning system for dangerous asteroids
summary: China revealed plans to construct an early-warning system for hazardous asteroids, including spacecraft to detect asteroids approaching from the sun's direction, a historically challenging blind spot.

title: Staffing the moon base: How many astronauts should live in NASA's lunar outpost?
summary: A simulation study suggests that mission design, not just psychological factors, is crucial for the success of NASA's future lunar outpost. The research explores optimal astronaut staffing levels for moon base operations.

title: SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket launches for record-breaking 36th time
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 rocket achieved a record-breaking 36th launch. The liftoff occurred at 5:25 a.m. ET on July 9.

title: Japanese company books 1,100 pounds of cargo space on SpaceX Starship mission to the moon
summary: Japanese company ispace has booked 1,100 pounds of cargo space on SpaceX's Starship mission to the moon, further expanding its lunar exploration plans.

title: NASA begins funding hardware for 'Skyfall' Mars helicopter mission
summary: NASA has selected Firefly Aerospace to develop the aeroshell for the 'Skyfall' Mars helicopter mission, part of its nuclear-powered Mars mission. The aeroshell will protect the descent stage during atmospheric entry.

title: Trump gifts Artemis astronauts an American flag from the US Capitol to plant on the moon
summary: Former President Trump gifted an American flag from the US Capitol to Artemis astronauts, intended to be planted on the moon. This flag was given to one of the last astronauts to walk on the lunar surface.

title: Shoebox-sized 'detector satellites' could sniff out a nuclear bomb in space
summary: A new study proposes using a constellation of cubesats equipped with special detectors to identify nuclear weapons hidden on satellites launched by adversary nations. These shoebox-sized 'detector satellites' could enhance space-based nuclear detection capabilities.

title: Super Typhoon Bavi
summary: Super Typhoon Bavi, the third Category 5 tropical cyclone of 2026, impacted the U.S. Northern Mariana Islands and Guam before heading toward Asia. The storm's trajectory raised concerns for potential disruptions in the region.

title: Students Connect NASA Science With Indigenous Knowledge to Study Coastal Erosion
summary: Students are collaborating with NASA to study coastal erosion in the Pleasant Point Passamaquoddy Reservation using both scientific and Indigenous knowledge. The project aims to address the rapid shoreline changes threatening the community's land and cultural heritage.

title: Hubble Captures Star-Studded Cluster
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of Messier 3 (M3), a massive globular cluster in the Milky Way galaxy. M3 is composed of ancient stars formed from the same gas cloud, making them similar in age.

title: NASA Transfers ‘Hundred Acre Wood’ to Patuxent Research Refuge
summary: NASA transferred ownership of 105 acres of wooded land at its Goddard Space Flight Center to the Patuxent Research Refuge, managed by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service. The land, formerly NASA Goddard’s Area 400, now forms part of the largest unfragmented forest block in the region.

title: NASA’s New Horizons Spacecraft Wakes from Hibernation in Good Health
summary: NASA's New Horizons spacecraft has woken from its longest hibernation period of nearly a year in good health. It is now ready to transmit science data from the distant Kuiper Belt beyond Pluto.

title: Artemis II Crew and Apollo 14 Moon Tree
summary: The Artemis II crew participated in the dedication of an Apollo 14 Moon Tree at NASA's Johnson Space Center. The tree is a second-generation loblolly pine species grown from seeds originally carried aboard Apollo 14.

title: July 2026 Satellite Puzzler
summary: The 'July 2026 Satellite Puzzler' challenges participants to identify the location of a satellite image and explain its significance. The event is designed to engage the public in satellite imagery analysis.

title: The World Cup From 250 Miles Up
summary: Astronauts on the International Space Station have photographed cities hosting the 2026 FIFA World Cup from 250 miles above Earth. This highlights the unique perspective of space-based observations of major global events.

title: NASA Takes Flight For America’s 250th
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman leads a flyover with his personally owned Northrop F-5 Tiger during the Great American State Fair on July 4, 2026, celebrating America's 250th anniversary. The event highlights the nation's historical achievements in exploration and spaceflight.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays. The company reported significant gains in Electron launches during this period.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: New launch pads are being developed at Cape Canaveral for Stoke Space and Relativity Space, contributing to the Space Coast's ongoing revitalization. This expansion highlights the growing activity and investment in commercial spaceflight infrastructure.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch after achieving key milestones. The project represents a significant step in commercializing low Earth orbit.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is accelerating preparations for Starship launches from Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year. The company and its contractors are actively working to meet this timeline.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar lander contracts as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative in June 2026. This marks a significant step toward establishing a sustainable human presence on the Moon.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral using a hybrid plan following a recent anomaly that caused significant damage during the New Glenn launch. The company aims to restore operations swiftly while addressing the incident's aftermath.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A rescue mission for the Swift observatory and the final launch of an Atlas satellite are scheduled this week. The launches are part of multiple orbital missions planned globally.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is set to air launch the Swift Boost Mission to save a NASA space telescope, marking its first mission since 2021.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, which will aid future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The data supports comparative studies between the two missions.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting a series of events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25). These events aim to inform industry about planned activities, priorities, and opportunities across ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: A new advanced robotic arm is being developed for space applications, aiming to enhance satellite servicing and maintenance. This innovation could significantly reduce costs and extend the lifespan of orbital assets.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: Proba-3's Coronagraph spacecraft and its ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are testing and validating materials for ESA's missions to ensure they withstand the harsh space environment. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with their contributions.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news headline 'Sensitive and sturdy' suggests a focus on resilient or high-precision space technology, though the context is unclear due to the lack of detailed information.

title: Blue Origin Seeks $10B at $130B Valuation, Reporting Shows
summary: Blue Origin is seeking $10 billion in funding at a $130 billion valuation, marking a shift away from Jeff Bezos' sole financial support. The move signals a transition toward external investment as the company grows.

title: Auxilium Demos Tech To Enable Sustainable Bioprinting in Orbit
summary: Auxilium Biotechnologies successfully created multiple types of organ tissues aboard the ISS, demonstrating scalable bioprinting technology in orbit. This milestone highlights potential future applications for sustainable bioprinting in space.

title: ispace Launches Lunar Integrator Service With Starship
summary: ispace has secured 500 kg of payload capacity on SpaceX's Starship for lunar missions, with launches planned no earlier than 2030. The Japanese company is offering a new lunar integrator service to transport payloads to the Moon.

title: Venus Aerospace Raises $91M Series B to Scale RDRE Production
summary: Venus Aerospace has raised $91 million in a Series B funding round to scale production of its Rotating Detonation Rocket Engine (RDRE) technology. This investment highlights a growing industry trend toward more efficient rocket propulsion systems.

title: FCC Poised To Advance Sat Licensing Reform
summary: The FCC is set to vote on the Space Modernization Order on July 22, aiming to reform satellite licensing processes. FCC Chair Brendan Carr announced the upcoming decision, which could modernize regulations for the space industry.

title: Transporter-17 Sends the First Commercial Nuclear Sat to Orbit
summary: SpaceX's Transporter-17 rideshare mission launched 81 payloads to low Earth orbit (LEO), including the first commercial nuclear satellite. The liftoff occurred at 3:12 AM from Vandenberg Space Force Base.

title: NASA Releases CLD Draft RFP
summary: NASA is advancing its plan to replace the ISS by releasing a draft Request for Proposal (RFP) for Commercial Low Earth Orbit (CLD) stand-alone habitats. The move follows industry demonstrations of viable human habitation solutions in low Earth orbit.

title: Exclusive: Ravee Optics Raises $6M Seed to Build Compact Laser Terminals
summary: Ravee Optics has raised $6 million in a seed funding round to develop compact laser communication terminals. Their technology aims to make laser communications more accessible by offering lighter, smaller, and scalable solutions.

title: The Biggest Fundraises of H1 2026
summary: The article discusses the largest fundraises in the first half of 2026 within the space industry, highlighting significant financial growth. It emphasizes the role of rocket launches in boosting payload capacity globally.

title: Inside the Deepening Ties Between Europe and the Middle East
summary: Europe's strained geopolitical ties with the US have led to strengthened space industry collaborations with Middle Eastern companies. This shift is creating new opportunities for partnerships in the space sector.

title: Impulse Space and Relativity Space Join the Field for Space Force NSSL Phase 3, Lane 1
summary: Impulse Space and Relativity Space have been added to the list of eligible companies for the U.S. Space Force's National Security Space Launch (NSSL) Phase 3, Lane 1 awards. The field now includes seven companies competing for these contracts.

title: Beyond Gravity Signs Gamechanging Contract with Ariane Group
summary: Beyond Gravity has signed a major contract with ArianeGroup, solidifying its role in the European launch supply chain. The agreement is one of the largest in Beyond Gravity's history.

title: Iceye Makes Major Move in German With New Business Entity
summary: Iceye has established a new business entity in Germany, based in Berlin and Munich, to expand its presence in the German space market. This move marks Iceye as the latest major player entering the dynamic German space industry.

title: York Space and All.Space CEOs Detail Resilient Communications Vision as Acquisition Closes
summary: York Space Systems completed the acquisition of satcom terminal company All.Space, aiming to create a complete tactical satcom ecosystem. The CEOs of both companies highlighted the vision for resilient communications following the deal.

title: CesiumAstro Files for 737-Satellite Synchronicity Constellation
summary: CesiumAstro filed an FCC application for a 737-satellite constellation named Synchronicity to offer reconfigurable connectivity for fixed and mobile users. The Texas-based company specializes in software-defined satellite communications technology.

title: NATO Allies Plan Hybrid HALO Constellation
summary: Eight NATO allies are collaborating to create a hybrid military satellite constellation named HALO to enhance NATO's space resilience and military capabilities. The initiative aims to integrate national military satellites into a unified system.

title: SpaceX’s Transporter-17 Mission Launches 81 Payloads
summary: SpaceX launched 81 payloads on its Transporter-17 rideshare mission using a Falcon 9 rocket from Vandenberg Space Force Base. The mission took place early Tuesday morning.

title: Space Systems Command Awards SES 5-Year Blanket Purchase Agreement
summary: The U.S. Space Force’s Space Systems Command awarded SES Space and Defense a five-year blanket purchase agreement for global Ku-band connectivity. The deal includes managed connectivity services.

title: An Expedition Across the Arctic-Space Connection With David Marsh
summary: David Marsh recently returned from an expedition to the Arctic to study parallels between the hostile environment and space. The findings will be discussed on the 'On Orbit' program.

title: Neo Space and Hughes Deploy 3 Ground Gateways to Support IFC
summary: Neo Space Group and Hughes Network Systems have deployed three new Hughes ground gateways in Saudi Arabia and Europe to support NSG’s in-flight connectivity (IFC). The announcement highlights the expansion of infrastructure for in-flight internet services.
